Test Case: Verify JWT token generation with valid email
Pre-Conditions: JwtUtil bean initialized with secret key and expiration time
Test Steps:
1. Call generateToken() with email "test@example.com"
2. Verify token is not null
3. Verify token format (header.payload.signature)
Expected Results: Token generated successfully with 3 parts separated by dots, contains email claim, expiration set to 24 hours from now
Post-Condition: Token stored in memory for validation

Test Case: Verify email extraction from valid JWT token
Test Steps:
1. Generate token for "user@test.com"
2. Call extractEmail() with the token
3. Assert extracted email matches original

Test Case: Verify token expiration check for valid token
Test Steps:
1. Generate fresh token
2. Call isTokenExpired() with the token
3. Assert returns false
Expected Results: Returns false indicating token is not expired

Test Case: Verify token validation with matching UserDetails
Pre-Conditions: Valid token and matching UserDetails object
Test Steps:
1. Generate token for "test@example.com"
2. Create UserDetails with same email
3. Call validateToken(token, userDetails)
4. Assert returns true
Expected Results: Returns true indicating token is valid for the user

Test Case: Verify token validation fails with mismatched email
Pre-Conditions: Valid token but UserDetails with different email
Test Steps:
1. Generate token for "user1@test.com"
2. Create UserDetails with "user2@test.com"
3. Call validateToken(token, userDetails)
4. Assert returns false
Expected Results: Returns false indicating token does not match user

Test Case: Verify OTP generation produces 6-digit code
Test Steps:
1. Call private method generateOtp() via reflection or public method
2. Verify OTP length is 6
3. Verify OTP contains only digits

Test Case: Verify BCrypt password encoding
Test Steps:
1. Call encode() with password "Test123!"
2. Verify encoded string starts with "$2a$" or "$2b$"
3. Verify encoded length > 50 characters
Expected Results: BCrypt hash generated starting with "$2a$" or "$2b$", length > 50

Test Case: Verify password matching with correct password
Test Steps:
1. Encode password "MyPass123"
2. Call matches("MyPass123", encodedPassword)
3. Assert returns true

Test Case: Verify password matching fails with wrong password
Test Steps:
1. Encode password "MyPass123"
2. Call matches("WrongPass", encodedPassword)
3. Assert returns false
Expected Results: Returns false indicating password does not match

Test Case: Verify Registration entity to DTO conversion
Pre-Conditions: Registration entity with all fields populated
Test Steps:
1. Create Registration with id=1, status=SUCCESS, seats=2, registeredAt=2026-01-15
2. Call toResponse(registration)
3. Verify all fields mapped correctly
Expected Results: RegistrationResponse with matching fields: id=1, status="SUCCESS", numberOfSeats=2, registeredAt=2026-01-15T10:00:00

Test Case: Verify available seats calculation with no registrations
Test Steps:
1. Mock seminar.getTotalSeats() returns 50
2. Mock registrationRepository.countByStatusAndSeminarId() returns 0
3. Calculate available seats
4. Assert result is 50

Test Case: Verify available seats calculation with partial bookings
Test Steps:
1. Mock seminar.getTotalSeats() returns 50
2. Mock registrationRepository.sumSeats() returns 30
3. Calculate available seats
4. Assert result is 20

Test Case: Verify available seats calculation when fully booked
Test Steps:
1. Mock seminar.getTotalSeats() returns 50
2. Mock registrationRepository.sumSeats() returns 50
3. Calculate available seats
4. Assert result is 0

Test Case: Verify ResourceNotFoundException handling
Pre-Conditions: Exception thrown with message "Seminar not found"
Test Steps:
1. Create ResourceNotFoundException("Seminar not found")
2. Call handleResourceNotFound(exception)
3. Verify ResponseEntity status is 404
4. Verify ApiErrorResponse contains correct message
Expected Results: ResponseEntity with status 404, body contains ApiErrorResponse with message "Seminar not found", timestamp populated

Test Case: Verify BadRequestException handling
Pre-Conditions: Exception thrown with message "Invalid seat count"
Test Steps:
1. Create BadRequestException("Invalid seat count")
2. Call handleBadRequest(exception)
3. Verify ResponseEntity status is 400
4. Verify ApiErrorResponse contains correct message
Expected Results: ResponseEntity with status 400, body contains ApiErrorResponse with message "Invalid seat count", timestamp populated

Test Case: Verify UnauthorizedException handling
Test Steps:
1. Create UnauthorizedException("Invalid credentials")
2. Call handleUnauthorized(exception)
3. Verify ResponseEntity status is 401
4. Verify ApiErrorResponse contains correct message
Expected Results: ResponseEntity with status 401, body contains ApiErrorResponse with message "Invalid credentials", timestamp populated

Test Case: Verify MethodArgumentNotValidException handling
Test Steps:
1. Create MethodArgumentNotValidException with errors on "email" ("must be valid") and "password" ("too short")
2. Call handleValidationErrors(exception)
3. Verify ResponseEntity status is 400
4. Verify ApiErrorResponse contains concatenated error messages
Test Data: Field errors: email="must be valid", password="too short"
Expected Results: ResponseEntity with status 400, body contains ApiErrorResponse with message "email: must be valid; password: too short", timestamp populated

Test Case: Verify customer lookup by existing email
Pre-Conditions: Customer with email "test@example.com" exists in database
Test Steps:
1. Save customer with email "test@example.com"
2. Call findByEmail("test@example.com")
3. Verify Optional is present
4. Verify customer email matches

Test Case: Verify customer lookup by non-existing email
Pre-Conditions: No customer with email "notfound@test.com" in database
Test Steps:
1. Call findByEmail("notfound@test.com")
2. Verify Optional is empty

Test Case: Verify email existence check returns true
Test Steps:
1. Save customer with email "exists@test.com"
2. Call existsByEmail("exists@test.com")
3. Assert returns true

Test Case: Verify email existence check returns false
Pre-Conditions: No customer with email "new@test.com" in database
Test Steps:
1. Call existsByEmail("new@test.com")
2. Assert returns false

Test Case: Verify duplicate registration check returns true
Pre-Conditions: Customer has SUCCESS registration for seminar
Test Steps:
1. Save registration with customerId=1, seminarId=5, status=SUCCESS
2. Call existsByCustomerIdAndSeminarIdAndStatusIn(1, 5, [SUCCESS, WAIT])
3. Assert returns true

Test Case: Verify duplicate registration check returns false
Pre-Conditions: Customer has no active registration for seminar
Test Steps:
1. Call existsByCustomerIdAndSeminarIdAndStatusIn(1, 5, [SUCCESS, WAIT])
2. Assert returns false

Test Case: Verify history filtering by date and status
Pre-Conditions: 3 registrations: 2 within 1 year, 1 older than 1 year
Test Steps:
1. Save registrations with dates: 2025-06-01, 2025-12-01, 2024-01-01
2. Call findByCustomerIdAndStatusInAndSeminarDateAfter(customerId, [SUCCESS, WAIT, CANCEL], 2025-04-24)
3. Verify returns 2 registrations

Test Case: Verify waitlisted registrations ordered by registeredAt
Test Steps:
1. Save 3 WAIT registrations for seminarId=5 with registeredAt: 10:00, 09:00, 11:00
2. Call findWaitlistedByOrderAsc(5)
3. Verify returns list ordered by registeredAt ascending

Test Case: Verify upcoming seminars query
Test Steps:
1. Save seminars with dates: 2026-05-01, 2026-06-01, 2026-07-01, 2025-01-01, 2025-02-01
2. Call findUpcomingSeminars(2026-04-24)
3. Verify returns 3 seminars
Expected Results: Returns 3 seminars with dates >= 2026-04-24, ordered by date ascending

Test Case: Verify OTP lookup with valid email and OTP
Pre-Conditions: EmailVerification record exists with email and OTP
Test Steps:
1. Save EmailVerification with email="test@example.com", otp="123456"
2. Call findByEmailAndOtp("test@example.com", "123456")
3. Verify Optional is present

Test Case: Verify OTP lookup fails with wrong OTP
Pre-Conditions: EmailVerification exists but OTP doesn't match
Test Steps:
1. Save EmailVerification with email="test@example.com", otp="123456"
2. Call findByEmailAndOtp("test@example.com", "999999")
3. Verify Optional is empty

Test Case: Verify retrieving all vehicles
Test Steps:
1. Save 3 vehicles with different makes/models
2. Call findAll()
3. Verify returns list of 3 vehicles
Test Data: Vehicles: Tesla Model 3, BYD Atto 3, Nissan Leaf

Test Case: Verify vehicle lookup by existing ID
Test Steps:
1. Save vehicle with id=1, make="Tesla", model="Model 3"
2. Call findById(1)
3. Verify Optional is present
4. Verify vehicle details match
Expected Results: Optional<Vehicle> containing vehicle with id=1, make="Tesla", model="Model 3"
